2604.08752.title: LLMs Underperform Graph-Based Parsers on Supervised Relation Extraction for Complex Graphs
2604.08752.paper_summary: Relation extraction represents a fundamental component in the process of creating knowledge graphs, among other applications. Large language models (LLMs) have been adopted as a promising tool for relation extraction, both in supervised and in-context learning settings. However, in this work we show that their performance still lags behind much smaller architectures when the linguistic graph underlying a text has great complexity. To demonstrate this, we evaluate four LLMs against a graph-based parser on six relation extraction datasets with sentence graphs of varying sizes and complexities. Our results show that the graph-based parser increasingly outperforms the LLMs, as the number of relations in the input documents increases. This makes the much lighter graph-based parser a superior choice in the presence of complex linguistic graphs.
2604.08752.paper_summary_zh: 關係提取是創建知識圖譜過程中的一個基本組成部分，還有其他應用。大型語言模型（LLMs）已被採用作為關係提取的一種有前景的工具，無論是在監督學習還是在上下文學習的環境中。然而，在這項工作中，我們顯示當文本底層的語言圖具有高度複雜性時，它們的性能仍然落後於更小的架構。為了證明這一點，我們在六個關係提取數據集上評估了四個LLM，這些數據集的句子圖具有不同的大小和複雜性。我們的結果顯示，隨著輸入文檔中關係數量的增加，基於圖的解析器的表現越來越超過LLM。這使得在存在複雜語言圖的情況下，輕量級的基於圖的解析器成為更優的選擇。
2604.08752.author: Paolo Gajo et.al.
2604.08752.authors: Paolo Gajo, Domenic Rosati, Hassan Sajjad, Alberto Barrón-Cedeño
